# Formula function reference

Every function available inside Flowfile's `[column]` [formula syntax](../concepts/expressions.md). These power the `flowfile_formula` / `flowfile_formulas` parameters on `filter` and `with_columns`, and the formula bar in the visual editor.

There are **95** functions across 5 categories. Use them with bracketed column references, e.g. `uppercase([name])` or `round([price] * [qty], 2)`.

## Logic & comparison

| Function | What it does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `between` | Checks if a value is between a minimum and maximum value (inclusive). | `between(5, 1, 10) would return True` |
| `coalesce` | Returns the first non-null value from a list of values. | `coalesce(null, null, "default") would return "default"` |
| `contains` | Checks if some text contains a specific pattern. | `contains("hello world", "world") would return True` |
| `does_not_equal` | Checks if two values are different from each other. | `does_not_equal("apple", "orange") would return True` |
| `equals` | Checks if two values are equal to each other. | `equals("apple", "apple") would return True` |
| `greatest` | Returns the largest value from a list of values. | `greatest(1, 5, 3) would return 5` |
| `ifnull` | Returns a default value if the input is null. | `ifnull(null, "default") would return "default"` |
| `is_empty` | Checks if a value is empty (null/missing). | `is_empty(null) would return True` |
| `is_not_empty` | Checks if a value contains something (is not null/missing). | `is_not_empty("apple") would return True` |
| `is_string` | Checks if a value is text (a string). | `is_string("apple") would return True, but is_string(123) would return False` |
| `least` | Returns the smallest value from a list of values. | `least(1, 5, 3) would return 1` |
| `nullif` | Returns null if the two values are equal, otherwise returns the first value. | `nullif(5, 5) would return null, but nullif(5, 3) would return 5` |
| `nvl` | Returns a default value if the input is null (alias for ifnull). | `nvl(null, "default") would return "default"` |

## String

| Function | What it does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `concat` | Combines multiple text values into a single text. | `concat("Hello", " ", "World") would return "Hello World"` |
| `count_match` | Counts how many times a pattern appears in text. | `count_match("banana", "a") would return 3` |
| `ends_with` | Checks if text ends with a specific suffix. | `ends_with("Hello World", "World") would return True` |
| `find_position` | Find the position of a substring within a string. |  |
| `left` | Gets a specified number of characters from the beginning of text. | `left("Hello World", 5) would return "Hello"` |
| `left_trim` | Removes spaces from the beginning of text. | `left_trim("  hello world  ") would return "hello world  "` |
| `length` | Counts the number of characters in text. | `length("hello") would return 5` |
| `lowercase` | Converts text to all lowercase. | `lowercase("Hello World") would return "hello world"` |
| `mid` | Extracts a portion of text from the middle starting at a specified position. | `mid("Hello World", 0, 5) would return "Hello"` |
| `pad_left` | Adds characters to the beginning of text to reach a specific length. | `pad_left("123", 5, "0") would return "00123"` |
| `pad_right` | Adds characters to the end of text to reach a specific length. | `pad_right("123", 5, "0") would return "12300"` |
| `repeat` | Repeats text a specified number of times. | `repeat("ab", 3) would return "ababab"` |
| `replace` | Replaces specific text with different text. | `replace("Hello world", "world", "friend") would return "Hello friend"` |
| `reverse` | Reverses the characters in text. | `reverse("Hello") would return "olleH"` |
| `right` | Gets a specified number of characters from the end of text. | `right("Hello World", 5) would return "World"` |
| `right_trim` | Removes spaces from the end of text. | `right_trim("hello world  ") would return "hello world"` |
| `split` | Splits text into a list using a delimiter. | `split("a,b,c", ",") would return ["a", "b", "c"]` |
| `starts_with` | Checks if text starts with a specific prefix. | `starts_with("Hello World", "Hello") would return True` |
| `string_similarity` | Measures how similar two texts are to each other, on a scale of 0 to 1. | `string_similarity("apple", "appl") might return 0.8` |
| `substring` | Extracts a portion of text starting at a specified position (alias for mid). | `substring("Hello World", 6, 5) would return "World"` |
| `titlecase` | Converts Text To Title Case, Where Each Word Is Capitalized. | `titlecase("hello world") would return "Hello World"` |
| `trim` | Removes spaces from both the beginning and end of text. | `trim("  hello world  ") would return "hello world"` |
| `uppercase` | Converts text to ALL UPPERCASE. | `uppercase("Hello World") would return "HELLO WORLD"` |

## Math

| Function | What it does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `abs` | Returns the absolute value of a number (removes negative sign). | `abs(-5) would return 5, and abs(5) would still return 5` |
| `acos` | Calculates the arccosine (inverse cosine) of a number. | `acos(1) would return 0` |
| `asin` | Calculates the arcsine (inverse sine) of a number. | `asin(0) would return 0` |
| `atan` | Calculates the arctangent (inverse tangent) of a number. | `atan(0) would return 0` |
| `ceil` | Rounds a number up to the nearest whole number. | `ceil(4.2) would return 5, and ceil(4.9) would also return 5` |
| `cos` | Calculates the cosine of an angle (in radians). | `cos(0) would return 1` |
| `exp` | Calculates e raised to the power of a number. | `exp(1) would return approximately 2.718` |
| `floor` | Rounds a number down to the nearest whole number. | `floor(4.7) would return 4, and floor(4.2) would also return 4` |
| `log` | Calculates the natural logarithm of a number. | `log(2.718) would return approximately 1` |
| `log10` | Calculates the base-10 logarithm of a number. | `log10(100) would return 2` |
| `log2` | Calculates the base-2 logarithm of a number. | `log2(8) would return 3` |
| `mod` | Calculates the remainder of division (modulo). | `mod(10, 3) would return 1 (10 divided by 3 is 3 remainder 1)` |
| `negation` | Changes the sign of a number (positive becomes negative, negative becomes positive). | `negation(5) would return -5, and negation(-3) would return 3` |
| `negative` | Returns the value -1. |  |
| `pow` | Raises a number to a power (alias for power). | `pow(2, 3) would return 8 (2^3 = 8)` |
| `power` | Raises a number to a power. | `power(2, 3) would return 8 (2^3 = 8)` |
| `random_int` | Generates a random whole number between two values. | `random_int(1, 6) might return 4 (like rolling a die)` |
| `round` | Rounds a number to a specified number of decimal places. | `round(3.14159, 2) would return 3.14` |
| `sign` | Returns the sign of a number (-1, 0, or 1). | `sign(-5) returns -1, sign(0) returns 0, sign(5) returns 1` |
| `sin` | Calculates the sine of an angle (in radians). | `sin(0) would return 0` |
| `sqrt` | Calculates the square root of a number. | `sqrt(9) would return 3` |
| `tan` | Calculates the tangent of an angle (in radians). | `tan(0) would return 0` |
| `tanh` | Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of a number. |  |

## Date & time

| Function | What it does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `add_days` | Adds a number of days to a date. | `add_days("2023-05-15", 5) would return "2023-05-20"` |
| `add_hours` | Adds a number of hours to a date and time. | `add_hours("2023-05-15 14:30:00", 3) would return "2023-05-15 17:30:00"` |
| `add_minutes` | Adds a number of minutes to a date and time. | `add_minutes("2023-05-15 14:30:00", 15) would return "2023-05-15 14:45:00"` |
| `add_months` | Adds a number of months to a date. | `add_months("2023-05-15", 2) would return "2023-07-15"` |
| `add_seconds` | Adds a number of seconds to a date and time. | `add_seconds("2023-05-15 14:30:00", 30) would return "2023-05-15 14:30:30"` |
| `add_weeks` | Adds a number of weeks to a date. | `add_weeks("2023-05-15", 2) would return "2023-05-29"` |
| `add_years` | Adds a number of years to a date. | `add_years("2023-05-15", 1) would return "2024-05-15"` |
| `date_diff_days` | Calculates the number of days between two dates. | `date_diff_days("2023-05-15", "2023-05-10") would return 5` |
| `date_trim` | Removes the smaller parts of a date or time. | `date_trim("2023-05-15 14:30:25", "day") would return "2023-05-15 00:00:00"` |
| `date_truncate` | Rounds a date down to the nearest specified unit. | `date_truncate("2023-05-15 14:30:25", "1d") would return "2023-05-15 00:00:00"` |
| `datetime_diff_nanoseconds` | Calculates the number of nanoseconds between two dates and times. |  |
| `datetime_diff_seconds` | Calculates the number of seconds between two dates and times. | `datetime_diff_seconds("2023-05-15 14:30:00", "2023-05-15 14:29:00") would return 60` |
| `day` | Gets the day from a date. | `day("2023-05-15") would return 15` |
| `dayofweek` | Gets the day of the week from a date (alias for weekday). | `dayofweek("2023-05-15") would return 1 (Monday)` |
| `dayofyear` | Gets the day of the year from a date (1-366). | `dayofyear("2023-02-01") would return 32` |
| `end_of_month` | Gets the last day of the month for a given date. | `end_of_month("2023-05-15") would return "2023-05-31"` |
| `format_date` | Formats a date as text using a specified format. | `format_date("2023-05-15", "%B %d, %Y") would return "May 15, 2023"` |
| `hour` | Gets the hour from a time. | `hour("2023-05-15 14:30:25") would return 14` |
| `minute` | Gets the minute from a time. | `minute("2023-05-15 14:30:25") would return 30` |
| `month` | month(date_value: Any) | `month("2023-05-15") would return 5` |
| `now` | Gets the current date and time. | `now() might return "2023-05-15 14:30:25"` |
| `quarter` | Gets the quarter from a date (1-4). | `quarter("2023-05-15") would return 2 (Q2)` |
| `second` | Gets the second from a time. | `second("2023-05-15 14:30:25") would return 25` |
| `start_of_month` | Gets the first day of the month for a given date. | `start_of_month("2023-05-15") would return "2023-05-01"` |
| `today` | Gets the current date. | `today() might return "2023-05-15"` |
| `week` | Gets the ISO week number from a date (1-53). | `week("2023-01-15") would return 2` |
| `weekday` | Gets the day of the week from a date (1=Monday, 7=Sunday). | `weekday("2023-05-15") would return 1 (Monday)` |
| `year` | Gets the year from a date. | `year("2023-05-15") would return 2023` |

## Type conversion

| Function | What it does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `to_boolean` | Converts a value to True or False. | `For example:` |
| `to_date` | Converts text to a date value. | `to_date("2023-01-15") would return a date value for January 15, 2023` |
| `to_datetime` | Convert a string to a datetime. |  |
| `to_decimal` | Converts a value to a decimal number with fixed precision. | `to_decimal("123.456789", 2) would return 123.46` |
| `to_float` | Converts a value to a number with decimal places. | `to_float("123.45") would return 123.45` |
| `to_integer` | Converts a value to a whole number. | `to_integer("123") would return 123, and to_integer(45.67) would return 45` |
| `to_number` | Converts a value to a number (same as to_float). | `to_number("123.45") would return 123.45` |
| `to_string` | Converts any value to text. | `to_string(123) would return "123"` |

---

!!! note "Fuzzy string matching"
    String-similarity functions (Levenshtein, Jaro-Winkler, etc.) require the optional `polars-ds` dependency and are not available in the browser playground.
